name: Qatar
summary: Day 71 Saturday. **QATAR'S TRIPLE-CHOKEPOINT (HELIUM/LNG/FERTILIZER) REMAINS BINDING DESPITE MOU FRAMEWORK; MARKETS STILL PRICING-THROUGH BUT BUFFER CLOCK ACTIVE.** **HELIUM:** Korean fab buffer 6-12 weeks per Smith/Discovery Alert puts absorptive capacity through ~mid-June (~5-6 weeks remaining as of May 9). Spot ultra-pure helium up 40-100% (~doubled per Fortune, J2 Sourcing). **The most TIME-SENSITIVE chokepoint in graph:** Korean fabs source 65% helium from Qatar; Samsung HeRS recycling system covers only ~18.6% of consumption. Cheniere CCO May 7: 'astounded that gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' **LNG:** European TTF €43.99/MWh (+0.98% 1D, ~-8% MoM off March peak, ~+40% from pre-war ~€31.20). 100 LNG cargoes/month disrupted per Cheniere; Qatar's structural role unchanged. **FERTILIZER:** Urea Yuzhny FOB $616.25/T (+6.71% May 6, +15.12% MoM, +81.69% YTD); CF Q1 EPS $3.98 vs $2.50 est (+59% surprise) but stock -3.1% on sell-the-news. Hormuz handles ~35% of global seaborne fertilizer trade; Cape rerouting +10-14 days. **Qatar's mediation role:** Doha continues supporting Pakistan as primary channel; Saudi via Al-Hadath May 8 endorsed 'Pakistan's efforts to broker a peace agreement' = Qatar's parallel mediation track now has Saudi public backing. The 'Qatar formally named primary mediator within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)' trigger from April 29 is at Day 10 of 14 — the Pakistan-led architecture has SIDELINED Qatar from primary mediator role. Qatar's structural relevance unchanged: (a) Hormuz reopening would unlock LNG/fertilizer/helium supply, (b) supporting-role mediation, (c) GCC alignment with Pakistan track.

historical_summaries.summary: 5 signals. QatarEnergy halted LNG production at Ras Laffan after initial attacks | Iranian missiles caused extensive damage at Ras Laffan — Pearl GTL facility and LNG Trains 4 & 6 destroyed | Qatar expelled Iranian attachés after Ras Laffan strike

20 signals.
